name: opsi
description: optimiziramsi's Claude Code toolkit — reusable skills, commands, agents, and hooks shared across projects.

plugins.name: git
plugins.description: Git safety net — a PreToolUse guard that blocks destructive/remote/history ops (push, pull, amend, rebase, reset --hard, --no-verify, discards) — plus a test-first hotfix skill (cherry-pick both ways, remotes user-owned). Commit style is the separate commit plugin.

plugins.name: commit
plugins.description: The opsi house commit style — bare imperative single-line messages, commit-as-you-go cadence, safe staging, a format guard, and an end-of-session nudge. Opt-in; pairs with the git safety net.

plugins.name: reporting
plugins.description: The lean-reporting output contract, enforced — contract injected per prompt, re-pulsed mid-turn, and a Stop guard that blocks a narrating/over-long final message. Opt-in, like the commit style.

plugins.name: session
plugins.description: Session continuity — write the handoff at session close, resume from it next time, and produce shareable session summaries.

plugins.name: review
plugins.description: Structured code/architecture review — review + qa-gate skills (P0/HIGH/MED/LOW findings to .agent/reviews/) + read-only audit agents incl. doc-auditor (docs-vs-code drift) and isolation-reviewer (multi-tenant isolation).

plugins.name: repo
plugins.description: Repo-maintenance utilities — rename a file with its full reference cascade.

plugins.name: instructions
plugins.description: Keep the agent instruction system alive — retro/lessons/audit skills + agents, a caps hook (instruction-surface size caps enforced mechanically), and a file-guard hook (edits to settings/hook guards ask the user).

plugins.name: flow
plugins.description: Work-management flow — plans, milestones, scope cuts, todo triage, a feedback ledger, and autonomous background execution (looper/grind runner); ships a todo-readonly-guard keeping .todo user-owned.

plugins.name: patterns
plugins.description: A per-topic pattern registry — author + curate blessed code shapes (manage-patterns), verify code against them (2 agents), and mechanically gate edits governed only by non-blessed patterns (hooks).

plugins.name: worktree
plugins.description: Git worktrees — the parallel-work protocol (review-gated slices landing to an integration branch) plus safety guards that keep edits inside the active worktree (mitigates claude-code #36182).

plugins.name: setup
plugins.description: One-time bootstrap — scaffold the .agent/ workspace index and point the entrypoint at it (scaffold), and write a house-style CLAUDE.md slim router of the hard rules that bind every session (scaffold-claude-md). No per-skill registration, no ongoing generation.
